This Council-funded initiative will provide free immigration legal services to NYC DOE students and/or their parents/guardians, particularly those at risk of deportation. Services include legal consultations, legal screenings, case management and immigration legal representation as determined by the demand. Organizations will work with public schools throughout the five boroughs to conduct school-based legal clinics, provide Know Your Rights workshops, application fees assistance, and provide legal representation. The funding may be used to support services provided in the client’s preferred languages. Funding may also be used to recruit, train, and supervise a cohort of School-Based Immigration Legal Access (SILA) Institute fellows, school-based Legal Representatives who seek DOJ partial accredited representative status, and School-Based Immigrant Rapid provide free immigration legal services to DOE’s students and their families.
